conditions.notes: Diagnosed at 62, treated with surgery + chemotherapy; in remission. Relevant to the differential for the patient's liver lesion (a metastasis would be on the differential).
Reportedly had 'a blood clot in the leg' in his 60s; details unknown. Raises the question of an inherited thrombophilia, though family detail is limited.
One younger brother (39), healthy. No known clotting or cancer history.

summary: Notable for FATHER with colorectal cancer (treated, in remission) — relevant because metastatic malignancy is on the differential for the liver lesion. A maternal grandfather reportedly had a leg clot, raising a limited question of inherited thrombophilia. No known first-degree VTE.
